words.def: to have such a strong emotional effect on somebody that it is difficult for them to resist or know how to react
words.example: She was overwhelmed by feelings of guilt.

words.def: very great or very strong; so powerful that you cannot resist it or decide how to react
words.example: The evidence against him was overwhelming.

words.def: a thick piece of soft material that is used, for example, for cleaning or protecting something or for holding liquid
words.example: medicated cleansing pads for sensitive skin

words.def: something that decides or limits the way in which something can be done
words.example: to set/define the parameters

words.def: connected with a parent or parents
words.example: parental responsibility/rights

words.def: an area that has its own church and that a priest is responsible for
words.example: a parish church/priest

words.def: connected with a parliament; having a parliament
words.example: parliamentary elections

words.def: not complete or whole
words.example: It was only a partial solution to the problem.

words.def: partly; not completely
words.example: The road was partially blocked by a fallen tree.

words.def: the process of time going by
words.example: The colour of the wood darkens with the passing of time.

words.def: accepting what happens or what people do without trying to change anything or oppose them
words.example: He played a passive role in the relationship.

words.def: a minister in charge of a Christian church or group, especially in some Nonconformist churches
words.example: He was ordained a pastor in the Lutheran Church.

words.def: a small area of something, especially one which is different from the area around it
words.example: a black dog with a white patch on its back

words.def: an official right to be the only person to make, use or sell a product or an invention; a document that proves this
words.example: patent on something, to apply for/obtain/take out a patent on an invention

words.def: a track that serves as a path
words.example: They came out of the woods and onto a pathway.

words.def: the act of going to different parts of a building, an area, etc. to make sure that there is no trouble or crime
words.example: Security guards make regular patrols at night.

words.def: a person who gives money and support to artists and writers
words.example: Frederick the Great was the patron of many artists.

words.def: the point when somebody/something is best, most successful, strongest, etc.
words.example: Traffic reaches its peak between 8 and 9 in the morning.

words.def: (especially in the past, or in poorer countries) a farmer who owns or rents a small piece of land
words.example: peasant farmers

words.def: strange or unusual, especially in a way that is unpleasant or makes you worried
words.example: a peculiar smell/taste

words.def: to continue to do something despite difficulties or opposition, in a way that can seem unreasonable
words.example: persist in doing something, Why do you persist in blaming yourself for what happened?

words.def: determined to do something despite difficulties, especially when other people are against you and think that you are being annoying or unreasonable
words.example: How do you deal with persistent salesmen who won't take no for an answer?

words.def: the people who work for an organization or one of the armed forces
words.example: There is a severe shortage of skilled personnel.

words.def: a written document signed by a large number of people that asks somebody in a position of authority to do or change something
words.example: petition against something, Would you like to sign our petition against experiments on animals?

words.def: a person who studies or writes about philosophy
words.example: We studied the writings of the Greek philosopher Aristotle.

words.def: connected with philosophy
words.example: the philosophical writings of Kant

words.def: a doctor, especially one who is a specialist in general medicine and not surgery
words.example: Dr Dennett is a practicing family physician in Atlanta.

words.def: a person who is the first to study and develop a particular area of knowledge, culture, etc. that other people then continue to develop
words.example: a pioneer in the field of microsurgery

words.def: a series of pipes that are usually underground and are used for carrying oil, gas, etc. over long distances
words.example: There are plans to lay a gas pipeline through the region.

words.def: (especially in the past) a person on a ship who attacks other ships at sea in order to steal from them
words.example: There were reports that a pirate ship had come looking for treasure in the cove.

words.def: a large deep hole in the ground
words.example: We dug a deep pit in the yard.

words.def: a serious emotional request, especially for something needing action now
words.example: plea for something, She made an impassioned plea for help.

words.def: to ask somebody for something in a very strong and serious way
words.example: plead (with somebody) (to do something), She pleaded with him not to go.

words.def: a serious promise
words.example: pledge (of something), a pledge of support

words.def: a small plastic object with two or three metal pins that connects a piece of electrical equipment to the main supply of electricity
words.example: The iron is fitted with a three-pin plug.

words.def: to move or make somebody/something move suddenly forwards and/or downwards
words.example: + adv./prep., She lost her balance and plunged 100 feet to her death.

words.def: a long thin straight piece of wood or metal, especially one with the end placed in the ground, used as a support
words.example: a tent pole

words.def: the process of questioning people who are representative of a larger group in order to get information about the general opinion
words.example: to carry out/conduct a poll

words.def: a small area of still water, especially one that is artificial
words.example: a fish pond

words.def: connected with modern popular music
words.example: a pop song

words.def: a thin flat case used for carrying documents, drawings, etc.
words.example: I left my portfolio on the train.

words.def: to show somebody/something in a picture; to describe somebody/something in a piece of writing
words.example: The painting portrays the duke’s third wife.

words.def: existing, happening or made in the period after a war, especially the Second World War
words.example: the post-war years

words.def: to arrange for an event, etc. to take place at a later time or date than originally planned
words.example: postpone something, The game has already been postponed three times.

words.def: a person who works in a profession, especially medicine or law
words.example: dental practitioners

words.def: to give a religious talk in a public place, especially in a church during a service
words.example: She preached to the congregation about forgiveness.

words.def: an official action or decision that has happened in the past and that is seen as an example or a rule to be followed in a similar situation later
words.example: The ruling set a precedent for future libel cases.

words.def: the quality of being exact, accurate and careful
words.example: Her writing is imaginative but lacks precision.

words.def: an animal that kills and eats other animals
words.example: Some animals have no natural predators.

words.def: a person who did a job before somebody else
words.example: The new president reversed many of the policies of his predecessor.

words.def: mostly; mainly
words.example: She works in a predominantly male environment.

words.def: the state of being pregnant
words.example: a pregnancy test

words.def: an unreasonable dislike of or preference for a person, group, custom, etc., especially when it is based on their race, religion, sex, etc.
words.example: a victim of racial prejudice

words.def: happening before a more important action or event
words.example: After a few preliminary remarks he announced the winners.

words.def: most important, famous or successful
words.example: one of the country’s premier chefs

words.def: a statement or an idea that forms the basis for a reasonable line of argument
words.example: the basic premise of her argument

words.def: an amount of money that you pay once or regularly for an insurance policy
words.example: a monthly premium of £6.25

words.def: to tell somebody to take a particular medicine or have a particular treatment; to write a prescription for a particular medicine, etc.
words.example: prescribe something, Valium is usually prescribed to treat anxiety.

words.def: an official piece of paper on which a doctor writes the type of medicine you should have, and which enables you to get it from a chemist's
words.example: prescription for something, The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.

words.def: at the time you are speaking or writing; now
words.example: The crime is presently being investigated by the police.

words.def: the act of keeping something in its original state or in good condition
words.example: building/environmental/food preservation

words.def: to lead or be in charge of a meeting, ceremony, etc.
words.example: the presiding judge

words.def: the job of being president of a country or an organization; the period of time somebody holds this job
words.example: the current holder of the EU presidency

words.def: connected with the position or activities of a president
words.example: a presidential campaign/candidate/election

words.def: respected and admired as very important or of very high quality
words.example: a prestigious award

words.def: used to say that you think that something is probably true
words.example: Presumably this is where the accident happened.

words.def: to suppose that something is true, although you do not have actual proof
words.example: They are very expensive, I presume?

words.def: to exist or be very common at a particular time or in a particular place
words.example: prevail in something, We were horrified at the conditions prevailing in local prisons.

words.def: the fact of existing or being very common at a particular time or in a particular place
words.example: an increase in the prevalence of smoking among young people

words.def: the act of stopping something bad from happening
words.example: accident/crime prevention

words.def: an animal, a bird, etc. that is hunted, killed and eaten by another
words.example: The lion will often stalk its prey for hours.

words.def: most important; main
words.example: The principal reason for this omission is lack of time.

words.def: the act of selling a business or an industry so that it is no longer owned by the government
words.example: There were fears that privatization would lead to job losses.

words.def: a special right or advantage that a particular person or group of people has
words.example: Education should be a universal right and not a privilege.

words.def: a complete and careful investigation of something
words.example: a police probe into the financial affairs of the company

words.def: difficult to deal with or to understand; full of problems; not certain to be successful
words.example: The situation is more problematic than we first thought.

words.def: the process of using a court to settle an argument or to deal with a complaint
words.example: court/legal/judicial proceedings

words.def: the money that you receive when you sell something or organize a performance, etc.; profits
words.example: She sold her car and bought a piano with the proceeds.

words.def: the treatment of raw material, food, etc. in order to change it, preserve it, etc.
words.example: the food processing industry

words.def: a machine, person or company that processes things
words.example: The company is Ireland's largest dairy processor.

words.def: to publicly and officially tell people about something important
words.example: proclaim something, The president proclaimed a state of emergency.

words.def: making goods or growing crops, especially in large quantities
words.example: highly productive farming land

words.def: the rate at which a worker, a company or a country produces goods, and the amount produced, compared with how much time, work and money is needed to produce them
words.example: high/improved/increased productivity

words.def: that makes or is likely to make money
words.example: a highly profitable business

words.def: very great; felt or experienced very strongly
words.example: profound changes in the earth’s climate

words.def: an estimate or a statement of what figures, amounts, or events will be in the future, or what they were in the past, based on what is happening now
words.example: to make forward/backward projections of population figures

words.def: important or well known
words.example: a prominent politician

words.def: very obvious, easy to notice or strongly expressed
words.example: He walked with a pronounced limp.

words.def: ideas or statements that may be false or present only one side of an argument that are used in order to gain support for a political leader, party, etc.
words.example: enemy propaganda

words.def: an idea or a plan of action that is suggested, especially in business
words.example: I'd like to put a business proposition to you.

words.def: to officially charge somebody with a crime in court
words.example: The police decided not to prosecute.

words.def: the process of trying to prove in court that somebody is guilty of a crime (= of prosecuting them); the process of being officially charged with a crime in court
words.example: Prosecution for a first minor offence rarely leads to imprisonment.

words.def: a public official who charges somebody officially with a crime and prosecutes them in court
words.example: the public/state prosecutor

words.def: expected to do something or to become something
words.example: a prospective buyer

words.def: the state of being successful, especially in making money
words.example: Our future prosperity depends on economic growth.

words.def: providing or intended to provide protection
words.example: Workers should wear full protective clothing.

words.def: a system of fixed rules and formal behaviour used at official meetings, usually between governments
words.example: a breach of protocol

words.def: one of the areas that some countries are divided into with its own local government
words.example: the provinces of Canada

words.def: connected with one of the large areas that some countries are divided into, with its own local government
words.example: provincial assemblies/elections

words.def: the act of supplying somebody with something that they need or want; something that is supplied
words.example: housing provision

words.def: to cause a particular reaction or have a particular effect
words.example: The announcement provoked a storm of protest.

words.def: relating to psychiatry or to mental illnesses
words.example: psychiatric disorders

words.def: the regular beat of the heart as it sends blood around the body, that can be felt in different places, especially on the inside part of the wrist; the number of times the heart beats in a minute
words.example: a strong/weak pulse

words.def: a machine that is used to force liquid, gas or air into or out of something
words.example: (British English), a petrol pump

words.def: a hard hit made with the fist (= closed hand)
words.example: a punch in the face

words.def: a question, especially one asking for information or expressing a doubt about something
words.example: Our assistants will be happy to answer your queries.

words.def: a long search for something, especially for some quality such as happiness
words.example: quest for something, the quest for happiness/knowledge/truth

words.def: a limited number or amount of people or things that is officially allowed
words.example: to introduce a strict import quota on grain

words.def: a system that uses radio waves to find the position and movement of objects, for example planes and ships, when they cannot be seen
words.example: They located the ship by radar.

words.def: relating to the most basic and important parts of something; complete and detailed
words.example: the need for radical changes in education

words.def: a feeling of violent anger that is difficult to control
words.example: His face was dark with rage.

words.def: a short surprise attack on an enemy by soldiers, ships or aircraft
words.example: to conduct/launch a raid

words.def: a large public meeting, especially one held to support a particular idea or political party
words.example: to attend/hold a rally

words.def: the position of somebody/something on a scale that shows how good or important they are in relation to other similar people or things, especially in sport
words.example: He has improved his ranking this season from 67th to 30th.

words.def: the crime of forcing somebody to have sex when they do not want it or are not able to agree to it
words.example: He was charged with rape.

words.def: the relationship between two groups of people or things that is represented by two numbers showing how much larger one group is than the other
words.example: The school has a very high teacher-student ratio.

words.def: based on reason rather than emotions
words.example: a rational argument/choice/decision

words.def: a narrow line of light, heat or other energy
words.example: The last of the sun's rays shone on the grass.

words.def: quickly and without difficulty
words.example: All ingredients are readily available from your local store.

words.def: the process of becoming aware of something
words.example: As realization dawned, he went pale.
